title: Replace raw wear proxies with a filtered latent degradation state
summary: Recent F1 tyre research shows state-space models with pit-stop resets outperform simpler autoregressive baselines and give interpretable latent degradation estimates.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre research shows state-space models with pit-stop resets outperform simpler autoregressive baselines and give interpretable latent degradation estimates. That is a concrete upgrade over hand-crafted `TyreLife` and `LapTime_Delta` ratios.
implementation_hint: Fit a lightweight per-stint filter offline: observed lap time as baseline pace plus latent degradation state with reset on pit stop and optional compound-specific transition rates. Export only a few derived features to the tabular model, such as filtered degradation level, one-step-ahead deterioration, residual-to-filter, and uncertainty proxy. Compare against the current best feature set rather than rebuilding the pipeline.
expected_effect: Should improve robustness when raw degradation columns are noisy, nonlinear, or distorted by driver error, especially near crossover laps where pit timing becomes ambiguous.
risk: More modeling complexity and potential mismatch between public-paper telemetry assumptions and this dataset’s columns. If the filter is badly estimated, it can add noise instead of signal.
